More specifically, it belongs to the genus Diploglossum. The species is endemic to the Himalayas and is also known as the Himalayan milkvine. The classification of Diploglossum roylei is further supported by its unique characteristics, such as its hairy stems and leaves. As a result, it is a notable species within its family and genus.
